title: plantago coronopus var. jacquinii
intro: Plantago coronopus var. jacquinii, also known as buckthorn plantain or plantain buckthorn, is a subspecies of the Plantago coronopus plant, a perennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Plantago coronopus var. jacquinii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health. Plantain buckthorn is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is also consumed as a food source, particularly in traditional medicine where it is valued for its nutrient-rich seeds.
